blocks.content: I felt it in two seconds: the difference between being somewhere and belonging somewhere.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh look at this place. Aye — the gym is down here, they got a weight room, the cafeteria's on the other side—

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: How do you know all that already?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Coach Murphy sent me a map over the summer. With notes.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: With notes. I told you I was going to be ready.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I'm in there too. Ms. Barrett. Come on.

blocks.speaker: Ms. Barrett
blocks.content: Find a seat. We start in two.
Middle row. Two seats in from the window. A girl was already there — natural hair, notebook already open. She looked up when I sat down.

blocks.speaker: Ms. Barrett
blocks.content: Alright. Before we get into anything — first day tradition. Name and one thing about yourself. Not your GPA, not your sport. Something true.
Eight kids before me. One said he played drums. One said she wanted to go to Howard. Someone said they liked hiking. Ms. Barrett said 'good' after each one, same tone, same beat.
Jonathan said 'Jonathan Michaels, wide receiver' and half the room turned. Ms. Barrett said 'one thing' without missing a beat. The class laughed. He sat down grinning.
Then Ms. Barrett looked at me.

blocks.prompt: The room is waiting. So is Ms. Barrett.
